summary: Code review of Phase 2 modularization (page extraction). BLOCKER: PostgreSQL not available (localhost:5432 connection refused). Critical bug found in exec() pattern that will cause runtime failures.

critical.issue: PostgreSQL not running on localhost:5432. App shows error on load preventing dynamic testing.

details: Router correctly checks aba value and calls appropriate module with {**globals(), **locals()} context
All 3 files exist with PAGE_CODE string containing extracted page logic

critical_code_review_comments: BUG: exec(PAGE_CODE, {}, local_ctx) uses empty dict {} for globals - this will fail at runtime
FIX REQUIRED: Change to exec(PAGE_CODE, local_ctx, local_ctx) or exec(PAGE_CODE, local_ctx)
Without fix: st, pd, json, time, logger and all app.py functions will be undefined
Affected files: map_page.py line 8, stock_page.py line 8, reports_page.py line 8
action_items: CRITICAL: Fix exec() pattern in all 3 module files - change exec(PAGE_CODE, {}, local_ctx) to exec(PAGE_CODE, local_ctx, local_ctx)
BLOCKER: Setup PostgreSQL or configure DATABASE_URL to enable dynamic testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 modularization code review complete. Database blocker persists (PostgreSQL localhost:5432 connection refused). Critical exec() bug found - globals dict is empty causing all imports/functions to be undefined at runtime. Fix: Change exec(PAGE_CODE, {}, local_ctx) to exec(PAGE_CODE, local_ctx, local_ctx) in map_page.py, stock_page.py, reports_page.py line 8.
rca_of_issue: The exec() function in Python takes (code, globals, locals). Passing {} as globals means built-ins, imports, and module-level functions are not accessible. The context passed from app.py contains all needed variables but is only used as locals, not globals.
